FlowX — Icon Inventory

Every icon in the FlowX designer with how to find it in the app and what it represents.

How to read each card. The line below the icon name is its semantic meaning. Below the divider, each entry names a feature area, followed by the step-by-step path through the live app and a final "Look for it" note describing exactly where on the page to find the icon. Generated 2026-06-07 from a static scan of the FlowX designer codebase.

For Claude — how to update this inventory in a future session

This document is self-rebuilding. Three machine-readable blobs are embedded below as <script> blocks (the browser ignores them) — they carry everything needed to diff the current codebase against this snapshot and regenerate an updated HTML without redoing the curation work.

#inventory-manifest — minimal snapshot: {group, name, usage_count, areas, sample_file} for every catalogued icon. Use this to detect what's new / missing / moved.
#inventory-curated — all curated content: PHOSPHOR_MEANINGS, CUSTOM_MEANINGS, SEMANTIC_PATTERNS, ROUTE_RULES (with navigation steps), ROLE_PATTERNS. Reuse verbatim; do not regenerate from scratch.
#inventory-build-script — the full Python generator that produced this HTML. Extract, write to /tmp/build.py, populate the intermediate JSON files it expects, and run it.
Step-by-step update workflow
  1. Read this HTML and extract the three embedded script blobs by id.
  2. Re-run the discovery sweeps the build script expects (phosphor name grep, SVG file enumeration, provideExtraIconSet registration scan, custom dictionary parsing). The script's loader expects these intermediate files: /tmp/phosphor_data.json, /tmp/flowx_svgs.json, /tmp/svg_usages.json, /tmp/custom_dicts.json, /tmp/custom_dict_svgs.json, /tmp/extra_icon_sets.json, /tmp/global_icon_usages.json.
  3. Diff the freshly-discovered icon list against #inventory-manifest.icons. Report to the user: added, removed, renamed, moved between groups.
  4. For new icons: ask the user for semantic meaning OR derive one from #inventory-curated.semantic_patterns. Add any new entries to PHOSPHOR_MEANINGS / CUSTOM_MEANINGS.
  5. For new modules / routes: extend ROUTE_RULES with a regex + area label + navigation steps. The steps array uses Markdown-style **bold** for UI labels (sidebar entries, tab names).
  6. Rerun the build script. It will write the new HTML and refresh the embedded blobs automatically.
Invariants — do not break these
  • Every preview tile is 48×48 with content sized to ~28×28 (or 40×40 inside the bpmn-tile / brand img path).
  • All icons inherit currentColor for fills/strokes except: designer_svgs (legacy thumbs), bpmn_editor (multi-layer compositions), logos — these preserve original colors and render as <img> from data URLs.
  • Every inline SVG gets ID + class namespacing to prevent cross-SVG collisions.
  • Transparent fills (rgba(R,G,B,0)) are preserved through normalize_svg_colors — they're typically click-target overlays and converting them to opaque currentColor destroys icons.
  • viewBox cropping uses _compute_svg_bbox which excludes invisible elements; it must only modify the root <svg> attributes, never inner shape attributes.
  • Each card has: a plain description paragraph (semantic meaning) below the icon name, then a single horizontal divider, then per-usage blocks consisting of a bold Area label and a numbered step list ending with a Look for it position hint. No "Source" file path, no "What it means here" duplication.
  • The "For Claude" section (this one) and its embedded blobs must be regenerated on every build so they reflect the current state.
Blob preview (first ~200 lines of the build script)
